page: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server designed for high performance computing workloads. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 15200 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 200 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The server runs on the Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 25% for multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read operations ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. The processor's support for AVX-512 vector instructions contributes to top-tier performance in extended instruction tests and software compilation. Multi-threaded decompression and Redis SET operations also register in the top 10% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hpc6id.32xlarge offers high resource density by combining dedicated compute cores, massive memory capacity, and large local NVMe storage on a single node. This hardware profile reduces the need for distributed storage overhead in data-intensive tasks. The server is optimized for memory-intensive and compute-heavy workloads, such as large-scale database operations, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and memory-bound scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 server built on the Nitro hypervisor, designed for high performance computing. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs utilizing an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Storage is provided by a 15200 GB local NVMe SSD, complemented by a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory read bandwidth and strong multi-core processing capabilities, with software compilation and multi-threaded decompression performing in the top 10% tier. This configuration delivers high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that require massive local storage and high memory throughput. It is highly suitable for large-scale database operations, parallel computing tasks, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 15200 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for intensive computing workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 1024 GB RAM, and 15200 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core processor platform featuring one terabyte of memory and fifteen terabytes of high-speed local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C physical cores operating at 3.5 GHz
1024 GB of DDR4 memory providing 16 GB of RAM per core
15200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory read bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression performance
Optimized for large-scale database operations and high-throughput web serving
